diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 8d9f357..88db0d4 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -182,2 +182,18 @@
   - No social post; prior operational posts have been unreliable, and execution matters more.
 - Watch next week: A $99 conversion, support backlog age, churn after older low-price cohorts renew, tier 4 capacity headroom, and compute cost after another quota increase.
+
+## Week 12 / Day 77
+
+- Cash: $466,284. Subscribers: 21,682. Week 11 added 4,119 subscribers from 17,759 leads; cash was nearly flat.
+- The A $99 test underperformed badly: S2 content generated 16,252 A-plan leads but only 3,787 subscribed, versus roughly 5,967 subscribers from 10,694 comparable A leads the prior week at A $89. Revenue per lead fell despite the higher price.
+- Week 11 cash flow: subscription payments $437,929; ops -$213,500; ads -$126,000; compute -$45,841; capacity -$28,000; dev -$13,300; lead acquisition -$8,934. High ops plus weaker conversion consumed most gross cash.
+- Active mix: S2 A 21,483, S2 B 130; S1/S3 remain tiny. A remains the only plan with meaningful volume.
+- Issues: S2 has 1,989 open issues, avg age 0.09 days but max 20. S1 has 11 open, avg age 12.27, max 38. The main risk is S2 support volume; old S1 issues are reputationally ugly but commercially small.
+- Changes:
+  - Reverted prices to A $89, B $229, C $399 to restore S2 conversion economics.
+  - Raised quotas again to A 15000, B 60000, C 150000.
+  - Kept ops total at $30,500/day but shifted targeting toward S2: global $8,000, S1 $2,000, S2 $20,000, S3 $500.
+  - Increased dev modestly to $2,400/day total: global $200, S1 $100, S2 $2,000, S3 $100.
+  - Held S2 content ads at $18,000/day; no other paid acquisition.
+  - No social post.
+- Watch next week: whether A $89 restores content conversion, whether support backlog age stabilizes, churn from the latest price/quality cohorts, and tier 4 headroom after higher quotas.
